objective: Keep Medusa as the current production-grade backend runtime while making future backend replacement or rewrite objectively possible through stable product contracts, bounded adapters, and migration gates.

currentBackend.principle: Use Medusa for commerce runtime, admin API, module loading, workflows, auth, and persistence today, but do not let product contracts require Medusa types or direct UI coupling.

principles.rule: Medusa remains the implementation runtime. Product-facing contracts must describe store capabilities, not Medusa API or ORM shapes.
A replacement backend must first satisfy storefront, admin, fulfillment, payment, inventory, recovery, audit, and migration contracts before any runtime cutover.
Existing Medusa coupling is baselined. New work may move coupling toward adapters, but may not increase the approved coupling budget.
Replace vertical slices behind compatible ports and shadow-read gates before considering a full backend rewrite.

Backend replacement is not ready until export, import, reconciliation, and rollback evidence prove carts, orders, payments, deliveries, credentials, customers, content, marketing, analytics, and audit data can survive the change.

storefrontBoundary.rule: Storefront UI must call the commerce port; Medusa URLs, publishable keys, and Store API paths stay in the adapter or server health probes.

adminBoundary.nextTarget: typed admin facade over the same-origin BFF
adminBoundary.rule: Browser admin code must never call the backend URL or handle Medusa auth tokens. Existing /admin/* literals are baselined and should be retired behind typed product-admin operations.

baselines.scope: Local-development baseline before cloud deployment or production operation.

backendMedusaImportFiles.meaning: Backend runtime files that import @medusajs/* outside tests.
backendMedusaImportFiles.exit: Product use cases move behind framework-neutral application ports; Medusa imports remain only in runtime adapters.

backendMedusaRequestResponseFiles.meaning: Backend API files that use MedusaRequest or MedusaResponse outside tests.
backendMedusaRequestResponseFiles.exit: HTTP handlers become thin Medusa adapters over framework-neutral use cases.

backendMedusaOrmFiles.meaning: Backend module files that use Medusa ORM/service/migration primitives outside tests.
backendMedusaOrmFiles.exit: Domain state has export/import and a non-Medusa persistence contract before backend replacement.

adminMedusaRouteLiteralOccurrences.meaning: Admin browser UI calls that still pass /admin/* Medusa route literals to the BFF helper.
adminMedusaRouteLiteralOccurrences.exit: Admin UI consumes typed product-admin operations instead of Medusa route strings.

storefrontMedusaEnvFiles.meaning: Storefront files that reference Medusa env names or Medusa backend URL symbols.
storefrontMedusaEnvFiles.exit: Only the Medusa adapter references Medusa-specific storefront env names.

maturityLevels.name: embedded-runtime
maturityLevels.description: Product behavior directly depends on framework routes, models, and UI-facing shapes.

maturityLevels.name: edge-ports
maturityLevels.description: Storefront and selected backend capabilities use ports, but admin and most backend flows still expose framework shapes.

maturityLevels.name: adapter-governed-runtime
maturityLevels.description: Current Medusa coupling is explicitly baselined, platform core is clean, browser direct coupling is forbidden, and CI blocks coupling growth.

maturityLevels.name: contract-complete
maturityLevels.description: Storefront, admin, payment, inventory, delivery, recovery, content, marketing, analytics, and ops contracts have adapter-agnostic fixtures and compatibility tests.

maturityLevels.name: shadow-replaceable
maturityLevels.description: A second backend can satisfy contracts in shadow mode with export/import/reconciliation evidence and no UI change.

maturityLevels.name: cutover-ready
maturityLevels.description: Runtime cutover has passed parity, data migration, rollback, health, performance, security, and operator workflow gates.

phases.goal: Make backend replacement readiness executable before cloud deployment.
Move admin UI from Medusa route literals and response shapes to typed product-admin operations and DTOs over the existing BFF.
Move payment, fulfillment, inventory, delivery, recovery, marketing, analytics, and content flows behind framework-neutral application use cases.
Create export/import/reconciliation fixtures and rollback drills for all product-owned state.
Run a candidate backend in shadow mode against the same product contracts before any rewrite or replacement cutover.

cutoverGates: No browser app imports or reads Medusa backend URLs directly.
Storefront and admin compatibility suites pass against the current Medusa adapter and a candidate backend adapter.
Product-owned data export/import/reconciliation produces zero critical drift on seeded fixtures.
Payment, delivery, credential inventory, recovery, audit, and support flows pass rollback-safe smoke tests.
Production readiness, security, CI, profile validation, runtime health, backup, and rollback evidence are green.
